title: Disk Full Service Outage
description: The internal employee portal is returning 503 errors. Multiple users are reporting the service is down. Investigate and resolve.

ticket.subject: [ALERT] Internal Portal returning HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable
ticket.body: Automated monitoring alert: The internal portal at portal.company.com has been returning HTTP 503 for the past 22 minutes. 47 users have been affected. The service health check is failing. Immediate investigation required.

system_facts.crash_reason: ENOSPC - No space left on device

logs: [2024-01-22 14:14:52] PORTAL: Writing session data... ERROR: ENOSPC - No space left on device
[2024-01-22 14:14:53] PORTAL: FATAL: Cannot write to /var/lib/portal/sessions — disk full
[2024-01-22 14:38:00] MONITOR: Alert triggered — 22 minutes of downtime

observations.check_logs:
APPLICATION LOGS (/var/log/portal/app.log):
[2024-01-22 14:14:52] PORTAL: Writing session data... ERROR: ENOSPC - No space left on device
[2024-01-22 14:14:53] PORTAL: FATAL: Cannot write to /var/lib/portal/sessions — disk full
[2024-01-22 14:14:53] PORTAL: Service shutting down unexpectedly
[2024-01-22 14:15:01] SYSTEMD: portal.service FAILED — exit code 1
[2024-01-22 14:15:30] NGINX: upstream portal:8080 unreachable — returning 503
observations.check_service_status:
SERVICE STATUS CHECK:
  Service: portal.service
  Status: FAILED (inactive)
  Exit code: 1
  Last active: 2024-01-22 14:14:53 UTC
  Restart attempts: 3 (all failed — disk still full)
  NGINX upstream: UNREACHABLE
  Health endpoint: HTTP 503
observations.inspect_disk_usage:
DISK USAGE REPORT for portal-prod-01:
  Filesystem    Size   Used   Avail  Use%  Mounted on
  /dev/sda1     200G   196G   4G     98%   /

  TOP DIRECTORIES:
  42G   /tmp
  18G   /var/log
  61G   /var/lib/portal
  35G   /usr
  40G   (other)

  WARNING: Disk usage at 98%. Service cannot write session data.
observations.identify_large_files:
LARGE FILE SCAN:
  /tmp/portal-upload-cache/    38.2G  (temp upload files, safe to delete)
  /tmp/crash-dumps/            3.8G   (old crash dumps, safe to delete)
  /var/log/portal/app.log.1    8.4G   (rotated log, safe to archive/delete)
  /var/log/portal/access.log   6.1G   (access log, can be compressed)
  /var/log/nginx/error.log.2   3.5G   (old nginx log)
  Total recoverable: ~60G
observations.clear_temp_files:
TEMP FILE CLEANUP:
  Removed: /tmp/portal-upload-cache/    38.2G
  Removed: /tmp/crash-dumps/            3.8G
  Total freed: 42.0 GB

  UPDATED DISK USAGE:
  Filesystem    Size   Used   Avail  Use%  Mounted on
  /dev/sda1     200G   154G   46G    77%   /

  Disk space is now sufficient for service restart.
observations.clear_old_logs:
LOG FILE CLEANUP:
  Compressed: /var/log/portal/app.log.1  (38.2G → 1.1G)
  Compressed: /var/log/portal/access.log (6.1G → 0.4G)
  Removed: /var/log/nginx/error.log.2   3.5G
  Total freed: 16.4 GB additional

  UPDATED DISK USAGE:
  Filesystem    Size   Used   Avail  Use%  Mounted on
  /dev/sda1     200G   137G   63G    69%   /
observations.restart_portal_service:
SERVICE RESTART:
  Command: systemctl restart portal.service
  Status: SUCCESS
  portal.service is now ACTIVE (running)
  PID: 14822
  Startup time: 4.2s
  Health check: HTTP 200 OK
  NGINX upstream: REACHABLE
observations.verify_service_health:
SERVICE HEALTH VERIFICATION:
  portal.company.com: HTTP 200 OK
  Response time: 142ms
  Session write test: PASS
  Active connections: 12 (users reconnecting)
  Disk usage: 77% (safe operating range)
observations.resolve_ticket:
TICKET-2047 RESOLVED:
  Root Cause: Disk reached 98% capacity due to accumulated temp upload cache (42GB) causing the portal service to crash with ENOSPC error.
  Actions Taken: Cleared temp files (42GB freed), restarted portal service.
  Recommendation: Implement automated disk cleanup cron job and set alert at 80% disk usage.
  Downtime: ~26 minutes
  Status: Closed
observations.unlock_account: INFO: Account lookup performed — no account lock issue found. This is not an authentication problem. Check service logs instead.
observations.reboot_server: CRITICAL WARNING: Full server reboot initiated. This caused additional downtime and affected all services on the host. Root cause analysis shows this was unnecessary — disk cleanup and service restart were sufficient.
observations.renew_ssl_cert: INFO: SSL certificate check — cert is valid for 89 more days. No SSL issue present. This action did not address the service outage.
observations.reset_password: INFO: No authentication issue detected. Password reset was unnecessary. Focus on service and infrastructure investigation.
observations.restart_vpn_gateway: INFO: VPN gateway is unrelated to the internal portal outage. This action had no effect on the reported issue.
